channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: All New Pokémon & Held Items in Pokémon Champions in Regulation M-B Update
item.content:encoded:
Major changes have been made to Pokémon Champions with the move to Regulation M-B, alongside the long-awaited release of the game on mobile, including several new Pokémon.

Launched on Nintendo Switch consoles earlier this year, Pokémon Champions is now the format for VGC events worldwide, and those competing during the Regulation M-B phase have been given even more Pokémon and Held Items to consider.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Regulation M-B New Pokémon

22 new Pokémon were added to Pokémon Champions following the move to Regulation M-B, not including various Mega Evolution forms which also entered the fray.

While the update, and simultaneous mobile release, was headlined by both forms of Mega Raichu, there’s still a wide array of Pokémon joining the battle, adding further depth to the available roster.

  • More Insider Gaming: Five Pokémon Champions Beginner Tips for New Mobile Players

New Pokémon include fan favorites from Gen 9, Annihilape and Gholdengo, along with the three starter Pokémon from the Hoenn region, Swampert, Sceptile, and Blaziken.

The table below showcases all of the new Pokémon added to Pokémon Champions with the Regulation M-B update on June 17, with thanks to Serebii.

Regulation M-B New Held Items

12 new Held Items were added to Pokémon Champions as part of the Regulation M-B update, not including the various Mega Stones required to Mega Evolve Pokémon.

This includes an array of items to boost weather-based teams, extending the duration of effects on the field, along with various boosts for accuracy, power, and more.

The table below showcases all of the newly added items to Pokémon Champions, including their effects.

Check out the Pokémon Champions mobile release trailer below.

Which new Pokémon and items are your favorite additions to Pokémon Champions? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, check out the hints of new content coming to Pokémon Pokopia, and don’t forget to sign up for the Insider Gaming newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Bungie Set To Lose Roughly 400 Staff In Summer Layoffs, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
Following the sunsetting of Destiny 2 and the generally underwhelming performance of Marathon, it seems that Bungie is set for a wave of layoffs this Summer, with at least 50% of its workforce on the chopping block.

The percentage is significant enough as is, but when you consider the size and scope of Bungie, which has around 800 staff members, that would mean at least 400 employees are at risk of losing their jobs.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Bungie On Track For Major Layoffs

The news comes from French journalist Sylvain on X, who shared in a recent tweet that Bungie should ‘expect massive layoffs this Summer.’ He went on to elaborate that, based on what he’s hearing, at least 50% of Bungie’s staff could be affected.

This all stems from the current state of Bungie’s two prominent titles: Destiny 2 and Marathon.

With regards to Destiny 2, it’s been a mess all around. Despite fan outcry, Destiny 2 received its final ever update, and there’s no sign of Destiny 3 in sight. Petitions may have been signed, and fans crashed the Destiny servers on the day of the update, but it doesn’t seem like enough for Sony to give Bungie the green light.

And then there’s Marathon. Critics may have reviewed it well, Insider Gaming included, and players who tried it have generally enjoyed the game, but that hasn’t been enough to stop it from being an underwhelming release.

  • Related: It’s Time For Destiny 2 To End, But Bungie Must Make Destiny 3

Add those two situations together, and unless there’s another game in development, there simply isn’t a need for around 800 staff members to work on an underperforming Marathon and a now-defunct Destiny 2—as has long been the rumor, layoffs could be inevitable.

It seems like it’s only a matter of time until we get the official announcement by Bungie and Sony, and it’s just another studio in what’s lining up to be a torrid Summer; Xbox is supposedly also planning a major wave of layoffs in July, whilst Ubisoft has already let go of hundreds of staff.

A horrible situation all around, but at least Destiny 2 remains playable. Check out the Monument of Triumphs trailer below, as it’s genuinely one of the best times to play the game—even if updates are done for good.

What do you think the future of Bungie looks like? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For your next read, check out the teaser for the new Pokémon Pokopia DLC features, and don’t forget to sign up for the Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.title: Gamers Are Convinced God of War: Laufey is Releasing Soon
item.content:encoded:
Optimistic PlayStation fans are convincing themselves that God of War: Laufey will be released soon, pointing to Sony’s heavy marketing of the title.

Revealed at PlayStation’s State of Play Showcase as part of Summer Games Fest, God of War: Laufey follows Faye, Kratos’ wife, in a spin-off title releasing exclusively on PlayStation 5, and some fans believe the release is right around the corner.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

God of War: Laufey Fans Convinced of Imminent Release

A quick search on X for ‘Laufey’ is all that is required to find fans stating their belief that God of War: Laufey could release sooner than anticipated, pointing to PlayStation’s main account changing the cover image to the game, and identifying how clean the gameplay footage from State of Play was.

Some have even gone as far as to suggest that the title could be shadow-dropped, similar to the Roguelite expansion to God of War: Ragnarok, though that seems extremely unlikely.

The fact that PlayStation is heavily marketing God of War: Laufey is not surprising, but regularly posting about the title and changing social media details to focus on the game is attracting a buzz, particularly with other major PlayStation releases on the horizon.

  • Related: PlayStation Plus Game Catalog Titles For June 2026 Revealed

Insomniac Games’ Wolverine is the first that springs to mind, set to release on PlayStation 5 on September 15, and PlayStation’s decision to seemingly focus on a flood of Laufey content over new engagement and building hype for the Marvel title has not gone unnoticed.

Still, there’s nothing to indicate that God of War: Laufey is releasing anytime soon, with the only reports so far on the release date indicating that it could be early in 2027 and will “definitely” be before 2028.

And while the gameplay footage shown at State of Play was stunning, it’s worth noting that it comes right at the beginning of the game, and will have been heavily polished, while development of later parts of the title could still need a lot of work.

Check out the reveal of God of War: Laufey and gameplay below.

When do you think God of War: Laufey will be released? Share your predictions in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, check out reports that XBOX revealed Senua to attract a buyer for Ninja Theory, and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Pokémon Pokopia DLC Listing Teases New Content & Features
item.content:encoded:
New details for the upcoming Pokémon Pokopia Expansion Pass have teased new content and features coming to the game during the three-part release cycle.

Announced during last week’s Nintendo Direct, paid DLC is coming to Pokémon Pokopia, alongside a free update for all players, introducing a new area and adding underwater exploration, and fresh details have now surfaced.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Pokémon Pokopia Digital Download Card Teases New Features

In anticipation of the launch of the first part of the new content, scheduled for August 2026, various retailers have posted images of the Digital Download Card for the Expansion Pass, which showcases new details.

One eye-catching addition is the tease of Snubbull, a Pokémon not currently available in Pokopia, which likely means its evolved form, Granbull, will also be added to the game.

However, potentially the most significant tease comes at the right side of the image, where Snubbull is featured, which shows the Pokémon and Peakychu both wearing attire.

The left-side of the image is clearly themed around the first piece of new content coming to Pokopia in August, with the introduction of underwater exploration, alongside new Pokémon in the form of Popplio and Manaphy.

  • Related: Pokopia To Get Free Underwater Update, Paid Expansion Announced

A new town, Bubbly Basin, is confirmed to be arriving as part of the paid DLC in August, but Koei Tecmo has already confirmed that Part 2, releasing later in 2026, does not feature a new town.

Instead, it appears the major feature introduced with update will be the ability to customize your favorite Pokémon in the game with clothing attire.

In the image, Snubbull is wearing a bow tie, while Peakychu is wearing wings and a hat. At the moment, it’s unclear whether specific Pokémon will have restrictions on the attire they can wear, and whether you can create custom designs.

While the news that there wouldn’t be a new town in part two of the Expansion Pass disappointed many fans, the tease of the new features showcases that there is still plenty to look forward to.

Check out the trailer for the Pokémon Pokopia Expansion Pass and the upcoming free update below.

Are you excited for the option to customize Pokémon in Pokopia? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, check out five tips for new Pokémon Champions players following the launch of the title on mobile devices, sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Pokémon Launches AI TCG Competition with $300k Prize Pool
item.content:encoded:
The Pokémon Company has revealed an AI competition, putting developers to the test by creating AI players capable of dominating the TCG scene.

The Pokémon franchise is in the midst of another huge bubble, sparked by the 30th anniversary celebrations, and a new competition with a prize pool in excess of $300k has been launched.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Pokémon Launches $300k AI Competition

After impressing in other tabletop games like Chess, The Pokémon Company is putting AI to the test in its trading game, introducing The Pokémon TCG AI Battle Challenge.

Taking place on Google’s Kaggle platform, the first round of the competition is now underway, with the top eight teams earning qualification for the Second Round and $30k each.

There are two categories in the first round, consisting of the Simulation Category, where “teams will submit their developed AI Training Agent for continuous automated matches on Kaggle’. Each team can submit five each day.

  • More Pokémon: Pokémon Moving North American International Championship to Chicago From 2027

Meanwhile, the Strategy Category requires participating teams to “submit a report detailing the strategic logic behind the AI Training Agent,” with rankings determined by “scoring the AI Training Agent’s stability, deck design concept, and Simulation Category Performance.

The card pool will consist of around 2,000 cards from the Standard format, and the final stage is scheduled for September 2026 in Japan, where AI players will compete in a live tournament that will be broadcast on the company’s official YouTube channel, Pokebeach states.

Check out the video of the announcement below.

What do you think about The Pokémon Company’s AI TCG Competition? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, check out five tips for new Pokémon Champions players following the launch of the title on mobile devices, sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/pokemon-ai-tcg-competition/

item.title: XBOX Revealed Senua to Attract Ninja Theory Buyer
item.content:encoded:
XBOX showcased the reveal of Senua during last week’s showcase in the hopes of attracting a buyer for studio Ninja Theory, a new report claims.

Ninja Theory is among several studios facing closure by XBOX, and while the closure decision surprised many as it occurred just days after the reveal of Senua, the apparent reasons for XBOX’s showcase decision have emerged.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

XBOX Hoped to Attract Ninja Theory Buyer With Senua Reveal

According to Game File, Microsoft’s plan to sunset or split with Ninja Theory was made ahead of the Xbox Game Showcase on June 7, with the thinking that “the promise of a newly announced game would help draw investor interest in the studio.”

However, it seems that it hasn’t quite gone to plan, as reports continue to indicate that Ninja Theory will be shuttered, while other studios facing closure have been reported to be in discussions to negotiate the prevention of being closed down.

Reports have stated that Compulsion and Double Fine are looking to negotiate with Microsoft’s gaming wing, but it has since been indicated that Ninja Theory is shutting down.

  • Related: Double Fine And Other Studios Reportedly Seeking Deals With XBOX To Avoid Closure

Ninja Theory’s closure and the other studios set to be sunset come amid a sweep of changes at XBOX under the leadership of Asha Sharma, who has also cut Game Pass prices and restored exclusivity banners for some titles.

However, one XBOX executive has said the company isn’t reversing course on its exclusive games strategy, despite Gears of War: E-Day and Fable confirmed to be platform exclusive.

Check out the reveal trailer for Senua below.

Is XBOX making a mistake shutting down Ninja Theory? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, check out the news that the entire Luna Abyss team has been laid off one month after release, and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: “It’s more like Vitality against Spirit than ZywOo against donk” – donk on Potential IEM Cologne Semi
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming spoke to Team Spirit rifling prodigy Danil ‘donk’ Kryshkovets ahead of the IEM Cologne 2026 Major playoffs.

donk gave Insider Gaming his thoughts on Team Spirit’s growth as a roster, the challenges of playing a Major without a coach, and his thoughts on potentially facing Vitality in the semis.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Insider Gaming interviews donk at the IEM Cologne 2026 Major

In Stage 2, you only drop 10 rounds, you know? Stage 3 was a bit more challenging. What were your reflections on Stage 3?

It has to be like this, like the stage three teams are stronger. Yeah, we faced 9z who we faced in stage two, but there was time to get some preparation and knowledge for them on how to beat us.

They did pretty well; they qualified for the playoffs. It’s actually like four teams from Stage 2 in the playoffs, so it’s insane.

So it has to be like this, and also, we were a little bit tired because we had a day off for like 20 plus days. Maybe there’s some problem in terms of fatigue.

After going through 3-0, do you feel refreshed going into the playoffs?

Yeah, we have like five days, I guess, until the match day, and this time we will spend on refreshment and some new strats preparation, and some time, we will try to improve our form. But the main thing here is about refreshment.

  • Related: Insider Gaming interviews Aurora’s w0xic at the IEM Cologne 2026 Major

Against 9z, you’re fatigued. It also felt like maybe, at times, there’s a bit of frustration. How do you manage that without having a coach behind you to take the negative emotions away?

Yeah, it’s tough. It’s really hard to get emotions off when you don’t have the person who usually does it.

When you lose control of the map, at like six to all or seven to all, five to all. When you have a gap in score, it’s really hard because there’s no one who can take a pause and say, ‘guys, now we do this, and we’ll destroy them.’

I think it’s the usual thing for the teams that make comebacks. That you’re taking a timeout, you win one round, you win the next one, and then there is already a small gap between scores. But we are trying our best.

Sometimes we are able to do it. Sometimes we are not, but we are doing our best. And I feel like in the playoffs, we all will be focused on it.

Since IEM Rio you have been playing a lot more complete Counter-Strike. What would you attribute that to?

I think from IEM Rio, we got this confidence that we’re still able to be in the finals against strong teams, and I think that gave us a lot of power and trust in each other.

That is the main thing. IEM Rio proved to us that we are still able, and we just started to work much harder, and that’s it.

In terms of magixx, do you feel like he’s more comfortable? Do you feel like he has a more complete playbook?

Yeah, definitely, now he feels more comfortable than he was. Every month and every game that we’ve played, he’s improving.

And you know, for me, a role IGL, it’s more about experience and just playing. The most experienced IGLs are the ones who have played for 20 years. So, he needs just time.

We all feel like he has everything to be a good IGL.

How do you think the team has changed to be less dependent on you?

I don’t, I think we had added some more moves, some individual moves that people want to do. And like, sometimes I throw flashes for my teammates, sometimes they do it.

Now everyone has their own space to make kills, and it makes this difference between the old team and this new team, I would say.

But I still have the same amount of kills because I always see the most enemies than my teammates because I’m the most active player/ I have the most active roles, and that’s it.

I’m not like in the middle of the map, there’s always a gunfight in my zone. So like, I will always have a kill, I would say.

  • More info: IEM Cologne 2026 Major Playoff Preview

In semi-finals, you might play Vitality, you might go like head-to-head with ZywOo, in that case, it’s the two best players in the world. Does that matter to you prove that you are the best?

Facing not ZywOo but Vitality is always motivating because like they’re, for me, they’re the best team in the history of CS. Prime Astralis didn’t have like this streak, a win streak like this, like Vitality has. I don’t think that Astralis was so dominant.

So it’s more, I think it’s more motivation about beating them than beating ZywOo, I would say. I don’t know why people comparing us because like I’m a rifler, he’s an AWPer. It’s not really fair. People can say that he’s AWPer because he’s like a hybrid, but like still, he usually plays with AWP.

So, it’s not fair. But it’s better to compare our teams. So. it’s more like Team Vitality against Team Spirit than ZywOo against donk.

Let us know your IEM Cologne 2026 Major predictions on Insider Gaming’s Discord


For other news, see the League of Legends MSI play-ins bracket and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
